title: My Dog Swallowed a Small Plastic Toy: Emergency Guide & Next Steps
meta_desc: Swallowed plastic can be dangerous for dogs. Learn what to do, signs of a blockage, and how to help your vet triage the situation effectively.
content_html:
TL;DR: If your dog swallowed a plastic toy, contact a veterinarian within the hour to discuss options like induced vomiting before the object moves into the intestines. Prompt action can prevent a life-threatening blockage and the need for invasive surgery.

What should I know if my dog swallows a plastic toy?

  • Foreign body ingestion occurs when a dog swallows a non-food item, such as a small plastic toy.
  • Plastic is particularly concerning because it is indigestible and will not break down.
  • Depending on the size, shape, and sharpness, the item may pass safely or become lodged, causing a gastrointestinal obstruction.

Is a swallowed plastic toy a veterinary emergency?

  • Urgency Level: Medium. Contact your veterinarian within one hour, even if your dog is acting normally.
  • Early Intervention: Certain items can be safely removed via induced vomiting or endoscopy while still in the stomach.
  • Risk of Delay: Once an object moves into the intestines, surgical removal often becomes the only treatment option.

Why should I show my veterinarian a photo of the swallowed object?

  • Visual Assessment: A photo of an identical toy helps the medical team determine dimensions and identify sharp edges.
  • Risk Evaluation: Knowing the material and shape helps assess the risk of a puncture to the digestive lining.
  • Diagnostic Planning: This information helps the vet decide if the object will be visible on an X-ray or if an ultrasound is necessary.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

If a dog swallows a small plastic toy, monitor for passage via serial abdominal radiographs if the animal is clinically stable. Failure of the object to pass within 48 hours, or any worsening of clinical signs, warrants intervention. Endoscopic or surgical retrieval is recommended due to the potential for obstruction or perforation. Exploratory laparotomy is indicated if the foreign body is distal to the pyloric region, if there are foreign bodies at multiple locations, or if there are signs of septic peritonitis, and the entire GI tract must be inspected for additional objects.

Chapter: Gastroenterology, Surgery

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 391)

faqs.question: Should I induce vomiting at home?
faqs.answer: Never induce vomiting without a veterinarian's explicit instruction. If the plastic toy is sharp or large, bringing it back up could cause severe damage to the esophagus.

faqs.question: What symptoms indicate a serious blockage?
faqs.answer: Seek immediate emergency care if your dog experiences repeated vomiting, loss of appetite, abdominal pain, or an inability to keep down water.

faqs.question: Can a small plastic toy pass on its own?
faqs.answer: Very small, smooth plastic objects may pass through the stool within 24 to 72 hours, but this should only be monitored under the guidance of a vet to ensure no internal damage occurs.
